qa_pairs.question: Who maintains the Subatomic and High Energy Physics Software page?
qa_pairs.answer: The Compute Canada Subatomic Physics National Team maintains the Subatomic and High Energy Physics Software page.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Subatomic and High Energy Physics Software page last updated?
qa_pairs.answer: The Subatomic and High Energy Physics Software page was last updated in July 2021.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a reference page available for the Astronomy and High Energy Physics Interactive Analysis Facility?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there is a reference page for the Astronomy and High Energy Physics Interactive Analysis Facility.

qa_pairs.question: What do many subatomic experimental physics groups rely on for software?
qa_pairs.answer: Many subatomic experimental physics groups rely on CVMFS repositories from CERN or the Open Science Grid, as well as specific repositories for each experiment.

qa_pairs.question: Why might the CCenv cause conflicts with subatomic physics software setups?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCenv, set up for regular users, can create conflicts because standard libraries are provided from the Compute Canada soft.computecanada.ca CVMFS repository using Nix and Easybuild, rather than having the software installed on the base OS of the compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: Where can ATLAS users find helpful information?
qa_pairs.answer: ATLAS users can find helpful information on pages housed on the TRIUMF twiki.

qa_pairs.question: What is a key recommendation for ATLAS users regarding Tier-3 setups?
qa_pairs.answer: ATLAS users should use the recommended setups for Tier-3 use rather than reinventing techniques described elsewhere.

qa_pairs.question: What is the URL for Compute Canada Tier-3 information relevant to ATLAS users?
qa_pairs.answer: The URL is https://twiki.atlas-canada.ca/bin/view/AtlasCanada/ComputeCanadaTier3s.

qa_pairs.question: What is the URL for container information relevant to ATLAS users?
qa_pairs.answer: The URL is https://twiki.atlas-canada.ca/bin/view/AtlasCanada/Containers.

qa_pairs.question: Do Compute Canada (CC) computing nodes have the HEP_OSLibs packages/rpms setup by default?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the HEP_OSLibs packages/rpms setup is not present on Compute Canada computing nodes by default.

qa_pairs.question: What is the suggested approach for setups when HEP_OSLibs are not on CC computing nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: The suggested approach is to use singularity containers that have the necessary rpms installed.

qa_pairs.question: What is an alternative, though less suggested, approach for simple setups?
qa_pairs.answer: For simple setups, one might be able to use the 'sft.cern.ch' repository.

qa_pairs.question: What benefit do singularity containers offer regarding OS bases on Compute Canada infrastructure?
qa_pairs.answer: Singularity containers allow the use of different OS bases, such as SL6, on the CentOS7-based Compute Canada infrastructure.

qa_pairs.question: How can one set up a CentOS7-based view from sft.cern.ch using gcc8 for LCG_95?
qa_pairs.answer: To set up a CentOS7-based view from sft.cern.ch with gcc8, one can use the command: `source /cvmfs/sft.cern.ch/lcg/views/setupViews.sh LCG_95 x86_64-centos7-gcc8-opt`.

qa_pairs.question: What software components are included when setting up a CentOS7-based view from sft.cern.ch?
qa_pairs.answer: Setting up a CentOS7-based view from sft.cern.ch includes necessary paths to compilers, geant4, ROOT, and other components.

qa_pairs.question: What are some available LCG_95 setups for x86_64 architecture with CentOS7?
qa_pairs.answer: Available LCG_95 setups for x86_64 with CentOS7 include x86_64-centos7-gcc7-dbg, x86_64-centos7-gcc7-opt, x86_64-centos7-gcc8-dbg, and x86_64-centos7-gcc8-opt.

qa_pairs.question: Can LCG_95 be set up with SL6 and different gcc versions?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, LCG_95 can be set up with SL6 using gcc62, gcc7, or gcc8 in both debug and optimized versions.

qa_pairs.question: Are there Ubuntu 18.04-based setups available for LCG_95?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there are Ubuntu 18.04-based setups available for LCG_95, including x86_64-ubuntu1804-gcc7-opt, x86_64-ubuntu1804-gcc8-dbg, and x86_64-ubuntu1804-gcc8-opt.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one find a list of rpms installed by HEP_OSLibs for CentOS7?
qa_pairs.answer: A list of all rpms installed via HEP_OSLibs for CentOS7 is available at https://gitlab.cern.ch/linuxsupport/rpms/HEP_OSlibs/blob/7.2.11-3.el7/dependencies/HEP_OSlibs.x86_64.dependencies-recursive-flat.txt.

qa_pairs.question: How many main repositories for HEP-related container images are mentioned?
qa_pairs.answer: Two main repositories for HEP-related container images are mentioned.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main repositories for HEP-related container images?
qa_pairs.answer: The two main repositories for HEP-related container images are from ATLAS and WLCG.

qa_pairs.question: How are these HEP-related container image repositories distributed?
qa_pairs.answer: Both main repositories for HEP-related container images are distributed via CVMFS repositories.

qa_pairs.question: Where are the ATLAS distributions of Singularity images documented?
qa_pairs.answer: The ATLAS distributions of Singularity images are documented at https://twiki.cern.ch/twiki/bin/view/AtlasComputing/ADCContainersDeployment.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one find single file packed ATLAS Singularity images?
qa_pairs.answer: Single file packed ATLAS Singularity images can be found at `/cvmfs/atlas.cern.ch/repo/containers/images/singularity/`.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one find unpacked ATLAS Singularity images?
qa_pairs.answer: Unpacked ATLAS Singularity images can be found at `/cvmfs/atlas.cern.ch/repo/containers/fs/singularity/`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the WLCG unpacked repository?
qa_pairs.answer: The WLCG unpacked repository is a development project that uses DUCC to automate publishing container images from a Docker image registry to CVMFS.

qa_pairs.question: What tool does the WLCG unpacked repository use to automate publishing container images?
qa_pairs.answer: The WLCG unpacked repository uses DUCC to automate the publishing of container images.

qa_pairs.question: In what formats are images published to CVMFS by the WLCG unpacked repository?
qa_pairs.answer: Images are published to CVMFS in a standard directory structure format for Singularity and a layered format for Docker.

qa_pairs.question: How can Docker instantiate images directly from CVMFS using the WLCG unpacked repository?
qa_pairs.answer: Docker can instantiate images directly from CVMFS using the graph driver plugin, as the images are published in a layered format.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one find additional documentation for the WLCG unpacked repository project?
qa_pairs.answer: Additional documentation for the WLCG unpacked repository project is available at https://github.com/cvmfs/ducc.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one find the list of images automatically published by the WLCG unpacked repository?
qa_pairs.answer: The list of images automatically published by the WLCG unpacked repository is available at https://gitlab.cern.ch/unpacked/sync/blob/master/recipe.yaml.

qa_pairs.question: Which specific ATLAS image is included in the automatically published list by the WLCG unpacked repository?
qa_pairs.answer: The `atlas-grid-centos7` image is included in the list of images automatically published by the WLCG unpacked repository.

qa_pairs.question: How can one add an image to the list of automatically published images in the WLCG unpacked repository?
qa_pairs.answer: One can add an image to the list of automatically published images by submitting a merge request.
